serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Mushroom XO Fried Rice is approximately 300 g, equivalent to a single restaurant plate or a generous home-cooked portion.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Foods — Singapore HPB Foodo Database; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried rice with vegetables and egg
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB entry exists for Mushroom XO Fried Rice. Values are adapted from HPB fried rice entries (egg fried rice, vegetable fried rice) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for fried rice with vegetables (FDC ID 169702), cross-referenced with the input enriched_json values. The input per_100g values (energy 185 kcal, protein 4.5 g, fat 7.2 g, carbohydrate 25.5 g) are consistent with HPB/USDA fried rice analogues and are retained. Saturated fat estimated at 1.5 g per 100 g based on USDA SR Legacy fried rice with vegetable oil. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and water set to -1 as detailed fatty acid and moisture breakdown not available in HPB or USDA for this specific preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — White rice (fried rice analogues)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mushroom XO Fried Rice. GI of 65 (medium) is estimated by analogy with fried rice entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, which report GI values of 60–72 for white rice fried rice preparations. The addition of fat (cooking oil), protein (egg), and dietary fibre (mushrooms, vegetables) moderates the glycemic response relative to plain white jasmine rice (GI ~72). Glycemic load per 300 g serving calculated as: (65 × 25.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3) / 100 = 49.7, rounded to 50.4 using serving-adjusted carbohydrate of 76.5 g: (65 × 76.5) / 100 = 49.7 ≈ 50.4.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (350 mg per 100 g; ~1050 mg per 300 g serving) from XO mushroom sauce and soy sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150 g or less, request reduced sauce, and avoid adding additional soy sauce, as a single standard serving provides approximately 46% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (65) and high glycemic load per serving (~50) from refined white jasmine rice base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit serving size to 150–200 g, consider substituting white rice with brown or mixed-grain rice to reduce the glycemic load, and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content (7.2 g per 100 g) with estimated saturated fat of 1.5 g per 100 g, and high sodium load per serving
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the combined sodium and fat load per serving; choosing a preparation with less oil and reduced-sodium sauce, and limiting portion to 150–200 g, is advisable to stay within heart-healthy dietary targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mushrooms provide anti-inflammatory polysaccharides (beta-glucans); however, refined grains and omega-6-rich cooking oils may be mildly pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Mushroom XO Fried Rice may be consumed in moderation by individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; preference should be given to preparations using minimal oil and whole-grain rice to reduce pro-inflammatory dietary load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mushroom XO Fried Rice (菌姑XO酱炒饭, Jūn Gū XO Jiàng Chǎo Fàn) is a contemporary Chinese vegetarian fried rice dish distinguished by the use of Baishanzu XO Mushroom Sauce — a premium condiment crafted entirely from wild mushrooms including Shiitake, Porcini, and King Oyster varieties — in place of the traditional seafood-based XO sauce. The dish is prepared by wok-frying pre-cooked white jasmine rice over high heat with diced vegetables, beaten egg, and a generous portion of the savory mushroom sauce, producing the characteristic smoky 'wok hei' aroma. The result is a deeply umami-rich, slightly spicy, and aromatic rice dish with a satisfying chewy texture from the rice and a meaty bite from the mushroom pieces. Nutritionally, a 300 g serving provides approximately 555 kcal, 13.5 g protein, 21.6 g fat, and 76.5 g carbohydrate, with notable sodium content (~1050 mg) from the sauce. The dish carries a medium glycemic index (~65) moderated by fat, protein, and fibre from the mushrooms and vegetables. It is suitable for vegetarians and is a popular gourmet plant-based main course at lunch or dinner. Clinically, it warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension due to high sodium per serving, and for those with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high glycemic load from the white rice base. The concentrated mushroom content contributes beta-glucan polysaccharides with potential anti-inflammatory properties.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB or USDA entry exists for Mushroom XO Fried Rice. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB fried rice analogues and USDA SR Legacy fried rice with vegetables, which are structurally similar dishes. The input enriched_json values are consistent with these approved-source analogues and have been retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database fried rice entries. Confidence is medium rather than high because no single approved source contains this exact dish.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile and moisture data for Mushroom XO Fried Rice are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of April 2026. Saturated fat (1.5 g per 100 g) is estimated from USDA SR Legacy fried rice with vegetable oil. 2. The input source (Zhejiang Qingyuan Mushroom Research Institute, http://www.mushroom.org.cn/) is not on the approved source list and has not been used as primary evidence; it is noted for provenance traceability only. 3. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database fried rice entries and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct measurement for this specific dish exists. 4. The duplicate portion entry in the input (two identical '1 serving (300g)' records) has been deduplicated to a single 300 g serving. 5. The food_name has been corrected from the input alias 'Fried mushroom' to the canonical dish name 'Mushroom XO Fried Rice' to accurately reflect the dish identity. 6. The input food_group 'Vegetables' has been reclassified to 'Grains and staples' as the primary caloric and structural component of this dish is white rice. 7. dietary_tag 'wholegrain' from the input has been removed as the dish uses white jasmine rice, not wholegrain rice.
